reasoning: The question asks whether Shakhriyar Mamedyarov's Elo rating on Wikipedia's 'FIDE rankings' page will be at least 1% higher than on 2025-11-09 across three dates. A 1% increase on a 2742 Elo rating corresponds to a gain of at least 27.42 rating points. In the FIDE rating system, a jump of 27-28 points for a super-GM rated above 2740 is incredibly difficult, requiring an exceptional tournament performance (e.g., scoring +5 or +6 against top-tier opposition) which is very rare, especially for established ~40-year-old top GMs whose ratings typically trend stable or slightly downwards. Furthermore, Wikipedia's FIDE rankings page generally captures FIDE's official monthly rating list (updated on the 1st of each month). Therefore, on 2025-11-16 (just 7 days later), the rating will almost universally be exactly the same as on 2025-11-09, strictly violating 'at least 1% higher'. By December 2025 or February 2026, he could theoretically gain the required points, but the base rate of such massive Elo spikes within 1-3 months is nearly zero. I assign the lowest allowable probabilities, increasing slightly for later dates to account for the longer timeline and increased uncertainty.
